name: Portainer MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Portainer MCP server enabling AI agents to manage containerized infrastructure via Portainer — querying and managing Docker containers, Kubernetes workloads, stacks, volumes, networks, and registries through Portainer's unified management interface.
use_cases: Managing Docker containers and services from infrastructure agents
Deploying and updating Kubernetes workloads from CI/CD agents
Monitoring container health and resource usage from ops agents
Managing Docker Compose stacks from deployment agents
Scaling services and managing replicas from autoscaling agents
Querying container logs for debugging agents
not_for: Direct kubectl/Docker CLI operations without Portainer
Teams using ArgoCD, Rancher, or other Kubernetes management tools
Production Kubernetes at enterprise scale (use dedicated k8s MCPs)
best_when: An agent needs to manage Docker or Kubernetes infrastructure in a Portainer-managed environment — especially for self-hosted or hybrid deployments.
avoid_when: You're managing Kubernetes directly or using ArgoCD/Rancher.

auth.notes: Portainer API keys or JWT tokens. Role-based access control at team and environment levels. Service accounts with minimal permissions recommended.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Portainer CE: free open source, unlimited
pricing.notes: Portainer Community Edition (CE) is free and open source. Business Edition adds enterprise features. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — no external rate limits; performance depends on Portainer instance

known_agent_gotchas: Portainer endpoint ID required for all container/stack operations
CE vs BE have different API capabilities
Container operations on Swarm vs standalone Docker have different semantics
Stack deployments use Docker Compose v3 format
TLS configuration is self-hosted operator responsibility
Portainer URL and port must be reachable from agent environment
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS configurable (self-hosted). RBAC with team/environment scoping. Self-hosted gives full data control. TLS setup is operator responsibility.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS configurable. RBAC. Self-hosted gives full data control. TLS is operator responsibility.
